caption: Security Finding
description:
Security Finding events describe findings, detections, anomalies, alerts, compliance evaluations, data security issues, and vulnerability assessments generated by security products. Use finding_type_id to classify the finding.

When to use this class: Use for all security-related findings regardless of subtype. Set finding_type_id to indicate whether this is a detection, vulnerability, compliance, data security, app security posture, or IAM analysis finding.
name: security_finding

access_analysis_result.description: The result of an IAM access analysis, evaluating who can access a resource.

anomaly_analyses.description: Describes baseline information about normal activity patterns, along with any detected deviations or anomalies that triggered this finding.

application.caption: Related Application
application.description: The application related to this finding. Populate for application security posture and similar findings; omit when not applicable.

applications.description: Details about applications, services, or systems that are accessible based on IAM analysis.

attacks.description: An array of MITRE ATT&CK® objects describing the tactics, techniques & sub-techniques associated to the Finding.

data_security.description: Data security context for data security findings.

database.description: Describes the database where classified or sensitive data is stored in, or was accessed from.

databucket.description: Describes the databucket where classified or sensitive data is stored in, or was accessed from.

evidences.description: Describes various evidence artifacts associated with the finding.

finding_type.description: The finding subtype caption, normalized from finding_type_id except when the value is Other.

1.caption: Detection
1.description: Detections or alerts generated by security products using correlation, detection engines, or other methodologies.

2.caption: Vulnerability
2.description: A vulnerability assessment identifying weaknesses in systems, software, or configurations.

3.caption: Compliance
3.description: Results of evaluations performed against compliance frameworks or security standards.

4.caption: Data Security
4.description: Findings from data security products such as DLP, data classification, or DSPM tools.

5.caption: App Security Posture
5.description: Application security posture findings from ASPM, TVM, or similar tools.

6.caption: IAM Analysis
6.description: IAM analysis results evaluating policies, access patterns, and IAM configurations.

identity_activity_metrics.description: Metrics about identity activity patterns relevant to IAM analysis findings.

is_alert.description: Indicates that the event is considered to be an alertable signal.

malware_scan_info.description: Describes details about a malware scan job that triggered this finding.

permission_analysis_results.description: Results of permission analysis evaluating what an identity can do.

remediation.description: Recommended remediation steps to address identified issues.

state.description: The normalized state of a security finding.

state_id.description: The normalized state identifier of a security finding.

1.caption: New
1.description: The finding is new and yet to be reviewed.

3.caption: Suppressed
3.description: The finding was reviewed, considered as a false positive and is now suppressed.

4.caption: Resolved
4.description: The finding was reviewed and remediated and is now considered resolved.

table.description: Describes the table where classified or sensitive data is stored in, or was accessed from.
